panels.title: Operator IPC (kernel ↔ CLI / dashboard / operator UDS)
Operator IPC: p95 latency (ms) by command_kind
panels.description: Per-frame operator UDS dispatch latency (kernel-side handler dispatch). Source: `OperatorIpcDuration` histogram. iter44 / INV-OBS-OPERATOR-IPC-COVERAGE-01.

panels.title: Operator IPC: dispatches total by command_kind / accepted
panels.description: Rate of operator UDS frames the kernel processed. Stacked by command_kind so the operator can see which command class is hot. `accepted=false` iff the response was `OperatorResponse::Error`. Source: `OperatorIpcTotal`.

panels.title: Operator IPC: rejection ratio (accepted=false / total), selected range
panels.description: Fraction of operator dispatches the kernel rejected. Sustained non-zero values point at a misconfigured CLI build, an epoch mismatch, or a fail-closed gate firing. Drill down into the `accepted` series above to see which command_kind is being rejected.

panels.title: Operator IPC: p99 latency (ms) by command_kind
panels.description: Tail latency for operator dispatches. Spikes here signal handler back-pressure (e.g. signature verification on the critical path of `approve_plan`).

panels.title: Kernel ↔ substrate IPC (planner / verifier / gateway frames over vsock)
Substrate IPC: p95 round-trip (ms) by role / message_kind
panels.description: Wall-clock from frame received → response written, per `kernel/src/ipc/server.rs::drive_planner_stream` arm. Source: `KernelSubstrateIpcRoundtripDuration` histogram. iter44 / INV-OBS-IPC-ROUNDTRIP-COVERAGE-01.

panels.title: Substrate IPC: messages, selected range by role / message_kind
panels.description: Frame throughput across the kernel ↔ substrate vsock chokepoint. Stacked by role so the operator can see whether planner inference frames, verifier witnesses, or gateway-fetch frames are dominating. Source: `KernelSubstrateIpcMessagesTotal`.

panels.title: Substrate IPC: in-flight frames by role (gauge)
panels.description: Per-role inflight gauge (incremented at frame-received, decremented when the handler arm returns). Persistent non-zero values mean the kernel is mid-handler on stalled frames — pivot from here to the kernel log to find the slow handler. Source: `KernelSubstrateIpcInflight`.

panels.title: Substrate IPC: p99 round-trip (ms) by role / message_kind
panels.description: Tail latency on the substrate IPC chokepoint. iter44 inference frames (planner/intent_request, planner/planner_fetch_request) typically dominate; tail spikes on verifier/witness_submission point at slow witness verification.

panels.title: Substrate IPC: counter ↔ histogram parity (per-role total diff)
panels.description: INV-OBS-IPC-ROUNDTRIP-COVERAGE-01 pins one counter increment + one histogram observation per frame — so the per-role totals MUST be equal. Sustained non-zero divergence on this panel = an emit site missed an arm or sample-rate kicked in unevenly across the two metrics. The RAII guard in `kernel/src/observability.rs::KernelSubstrateIpcRoundtrip::Drop` is the structural enforcement; this panel is the dashboard-side visual witness.
